questions.question: India became independent on _____ from British rule.
questions.explanation: India gained independence from British rule on 15 August 1947.

questions.question: The Constitution of India was framed by the _____.
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was the elected body that framed the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: _____ was the Chairman of the Constituent Assembly.
questions.explanation: Dr Rajendra Prasad was elected as the Chairman of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.question: Dr B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the _____ of the Constitution.
questions.explanation: Dr B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee, which was responsible for drafting the Constitution.

questions.question: The Constituent Assembly held its first meeting on _____.
questions.explanation: The first meeting of the Constituent Assembly took place on 9 December 1946.

questions.question: The Constituent Assembly took _____ to frame the Constitution.
questions.explanation: It took the Constituent Assembly 2 years, 11 months and 18 days to complete the framing of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Constitution of India was passed on _____.
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly passed the Constitution on 26 November 1949.

questions.question: The Constitution of India came into force on _____.
questions.explanation: The Constitution came into force on 26 January 1950, which is celebrated every year as Republic Day.

questions.question: 26 January is celebrated every year as _____.
questions.explanation: Republic Day is celebrated on 26 January because the Constitution came into force on this day in 1950.

questions.question: The Preamble describes India as a Sovereign _____ Secular Democratic Republic.
questions.explanation: The full description is Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic. Socialist means fair distribution of wealth.

questions.question: The words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' were added to the Preamble by the _____ Amendment in 1976.
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976 added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble.

questions.question: _____ means that India is independent and free from foreign control.
questions.explanation: Sovereign means India is an independent country, free from any foreign control or interference.

questions.question: _____ means the state has no official religion and does not discriminate on the basis of religion.
questions.explanation: Secular means there is no official state religion and the government treats all religions equally without discrimination.

questions.question: In a Republic, the head of the state is _____, not hereditary.
questions.explanation: Republic means the head of state (President) is elected by the people's representatives, unlike a monarchy where the position is hereditary.

questions.question: The President of India is elected for a term of _____ years.
questions.explanation: The President of India serves a term of 5 years and the position is not hereditary.

questions.question: The Preamble begins with the words '_____' indicating that the source of authority is the people.
questions.explanation: The opening words 'We, the people of India' establish that the ultimate authority and power rests with the citizens of India.

questions.question: The four objectives of the Preamble are Justice, Liberty, Equality and _____.
questions.explanation: The Preamble outlines four key objectives: Justice, Liberty, Equality and Fraternity (brotherhood).

questions.question: _____ means brotherhood among citizens and aims at removing social barriers.
questions.explanation: Fraternity refers to a spirit of brotherhood among all citizens, promoting unity and removing social barriers.

questions.question: Dr B.R. Ambedkar is known as the '_____'.
questions.explanation: Dr B.R. Ambedkar is called the Father of the Indian Constitution because he chaired the Drafting Committee that prepared the Constitution.

questions.question: The original copy of the Indian Constitution is kept in a _____ case in the Library of Parliament.
questions.explanation: The original handwritten copy of the Constitution is preserved in a special helium-filled case in the Library of Parliament to prevent deterioration.
